actual.name: actual
actual.summary: A session-level orchestration loop sits atop a shared noticeboard where AI agents deposit findings. A skeleton-builder detects structural connections between findings; a doubt meter tracks diminishing returns; two independent gap-detectors (one goal-blind, one goal-aware) identify missing structural pieces. The cycle orchestrator must fuse these gap signals, decide how many agents to dispatch per gap, reframe exploration leads so they stay divergent, re-assemble the skeleton after each wave, and halt when the skeleton is complete or returns dry up — all without human intervention.

node_records.embedding_text: a common surface where distributed contributors deposit partial results that accumulate into a shared picture

search_queries: incremental graph construction from partial data
node_records.embedding_text: a structural scaffold assembled in real time by detecting which partial contributions share deep architecture
two independent diagnostic systems — one blind to the goal, one aware of it — that identify missing structural pieces from different vantage points
a decision layer that fuses multiple gap signals and determines how many explorers to send at each identified hole

search_queries: distinguishing true absence from insufficient sampling
node_records.embedding_text: distinguishing a hole that genuinely has no available content from one that simply has not yet received sufficient exploration effort

search_queries: goal-free divergent search from specific gap
reframing targeted query for broad discovery
node_records.embedding_text: converting a goal-specific blind spot into a domain-neutral exploration prompt so the next wave stays divergent rather than confirmatory
a convergence signal that stops the loop when successive cycles add less structural mass than a threshold, regardless of remaining budget

essence.name: essence
essence.summary: A self-directing system must measure its own incompleteness, decide where that incompleteness is worth chasing, allocate scarce effort toward the highest-leverage holes, and know when to stop — all while keeping its exploratory moves genuinely open rather than collapsing into self-confirmation. The core tension is between structural closure (the skeleton wants to be complete) and epistemic openness (the search must not be steered by what the skeleton already expects). A second tension runs between the two gap-detectors: one sees shape, one sees purpose — fusing them without letting either dominate is the allocation problem.

node_records.embedding_text: a system that continuously audits its own structural gaps rather than waiting for an external evaluator to declare it unfinished
the tension between a structure's pull toward completion and the requirement that the search feeding it remain undirected by what the structure already contains
combining two evaluations that use different criteria — structural shape versus goal relevance — into a single actionable priority without collapsing either signal
deciding how much resource to invest in a gap whose depth and fillability are themselves unknown at the time of dispatch

search_queries: distinguishing empty domain from undersampled domain
node_records.embedding_text: the diagnostic distinction between a gap that cannot be filled because the content does not exist and one that has simply not been searched hard enough
temporarily twisting the system's frame toward a specific gap while preserving the divergence that makes the search valuable in the first place
a halt that is justified by evidence of structural saturation rather than by budget exhaustion or arbitrary iteration count

mechanism.name: mechanism
nodes: undirected input requires directed gap-filling without collapsing undirectedness
disagreement between diagnostics marks the productive frontier
absence signal requires provenance to be interpretable
marginal structural gain as the correct stopping metric
allocation is a function of gap geometry, not gap count
mechanism.summary: A system that must produce emergent structure from distributed, undirected inputs faces a fundamental causal problem: the inputs are most valuable when they are not steered, but the structure can only close if effort is eventually directed at its holes. The resolution requires a meta-layer that operates on the structure's shape rather than its content — reading gaps as signals for where to redirect effort, translating those signals into content-neutral search commands so the next inputs remain genuinely surprising, and applying a stopping rule that is sensitive to marginal structural gain rather than raw output volume. The allocation sub-problem is itself a causal primitive: when two independent diagnostics disagree about priority, the disagreement itself is information — it marks the boundary between what the structure can predict and what it cannot, which is precisely where the next wave should go.

node_records.embedding_text: the partial structure produced so far is itself the most reliable instrument for detecting what is missing, because only a structure can have a structurally defined hole

node_records.text: undirected input requires directed gap-filling without collapsing undirectedness
search_queries: avoiding confirmation collapse in iterative discovery
node_records.embedding_text: systems that depend on divergent exploration for their value must translate structural gaps into content-neutral prompts or the next round of exploration degenerates into confirmation

node_records.text: disagreement between diagnostics marks the productive frontier
node_records.embedding_text: when a goal-blind detector and a goal-aware detector disagree on a gap's priority, that disagreement identifies the region the current structure cannot yet predict — the highest-information zone for next exploration

node_records.text: absence signal requires provenance to be interpretable
search_queries: distinguishing no evidence from evidence of absence
node_records.embedding_text: a null return from exploration is only meaningful as a stopping signal if the system can verify that effort was actually directed at the gap; unverified absence cannot be distinguished from unsent effort

node_records.text: marginal structural gain as the correct stopping metric
search_queries: marginal gain stopping rule structure building
node_records.embedding_text: halting rules calibrated to output volume or budget are systematically wrong for structure-building tasks; the correct metric is the increment of structural mass added per cycle, which naturally reaches zero at saturation

node_records.text: allocation is a function of gap geometry, not gap count
node_records.embedding_text: the right number of agents to dispatch at a gap is determined by the gap's structural position — how many other nodes depend on it, how isolated it is — not by how many gaps exist in total

problem.name: problem
problem.content: I've built the first organ of Stage 2 — a session-level flow governor that watches findings arrive on the shared noticeboard, draws a live skeleton by detecting which findings share a deep structure, runs a diminishing-returns doubt meter, and can halt the wander early on convergence. I've validated the two load-bearing parts: the edge-detector discriminates real cross-connections from false ones, and a gap-namer can identify the structural pieces that are missing and corroborate an independent auditor's blind spots. What I'm designing now is the CYCLE ORCHESTRATOR that turns this watcher-with-an-off-switch into a self-directing loop: wander, then audit for blind spots, then rank the gaps, then dispatch a fresh wave of agents to fill them, then re-assemble the skeleton, and repeat until the structure is complete or the returns dry up. How that orchestration is shaped is what I'm trying to design.

vision.name: vision
vision.content: An AUTONOMOUS draft-maker. Instead of stopping at a token budget, the pipeline notices its own structural gaps, decides which to chase and with how many agents, re-routes itself toward them with no human in the loop, and stops when the skeleton meets the checkpoint or returns diminish — producing a finished concept-draft a human then judges. The human owns QUALITY; the pipeline owns FLOW. Every cycle it may temporarily TWIST its own framing toward an identified gap, chase it, and fold what comes back into the growing structure — a real step toward a concept advancing itself end to end.

hunches.name: hunches
hunches.content: The governor governs flow only, never quality — so the hard part is the ALLOCATION brain. Open questions I can't yet shape: how to fuse two independent gap-detectors (a goal-blind structural piece-finder and a goal-aware auditor) into one dispatch priority; how to decide the agent count per cycle (two versus all) from the gap structure; how to tell an UNFILLABLE gap (stop) from an UNDER-ATTEMPTED one (re-command deeper) — I suspect that's readable from whether agents were actually sent at it and came back empty; and how to convert goal-relative blind spots into goal-FREE exploration leads so re-wandering stays divergent and never collapses into confirming the question. Related territory: control theory and convergence/halting, multi-agent task allocation, active learning and experiment design, percolation and graph completeness, and the explore-exploit tradeoff.

question.name: question
question.content: How should an autonomous multi-agent system, after a first divergent search pass, ORCHESTRATE its own next passes? Specifically: (1) how to fuse multiple independent gap or uncertainty signals into a single allocation priority; (2) how to decide how many agents to commit to a given gap, and when; (3) how to distinguish an unfillable gap (terminate) from an under-attempted one (re-route deeper) from observable signals; (4) how to bias re-exploration toward identified gaps WITHOUT collapsing the diversity that made the first pass productive; and (5) what governs the overall STOP decision — coverage-complete versus diminishing-returns versus a hard cap — with no quality judge in the loop? What do control theory, active learning, multi-agent allocation, and scientific-discovery loops reveal about each?
